articles.title: Why We Choose NOT To Take The Step Towards Better
articles.excerpt: Fear and faith often sit on opposite ends of the same spectrum. Where fear says, "What if God doesn't?" -- faith says, "Even if He doesn't, I trust Him." The opposite of fear isn't bravery. It's trust.

articles.title: Not Cats No Cradle, No Regrets
articles.excerpt: As a son and now a father of three, I sometimes think about the moments we almost missed. The backyard baseball games. The jungle gyms. The walks that went nowhere in particular. Presence isn't a virtue -- it's a choice you make every single day.

articles.title: What Happened to My Friends?
articles.excerpt: You live a fulfilling life so far. You're in your mid-40s. You look back and realize you had all of these friends. Life gets busy. You build a family. You buy a home. And somewhere in there, the friendships quietly faded.

articles.title: The Art of Learning
articles.excerpt: When is the right time to pick up a new skill? I wanted to learn how to play a certain instrument, but I always felt like it was too late. Moments in our life are all relative to each other.

articles.title: Basketball Player / But Not a Real One
articles.excerpt: I have always loved the game. Jordan years. At the ripe age of 46, I started playing for real.

articles.title: Accepting Invitations
articles.excerpt: To speak. To connect. To hear a pitch. To partner. To mentor. Why I say yes -- and the three reasons that make it worth it.

articles.title: Brand -- Start by Looking Inward
articles.excerpt: It already exists. Clarity isn't about reinventing yourself -- it's about recognizing what's already unique. The most powerful brands don't get built from the outside in.

articles.title: Vision vs. Execution
articles.excerpt: The art and science of knowing when to dream and when to build. All mediums, all mindsets -- and why the gap between them is where most great ideas quietly disappear.

articles.title: Basketball Diaries
articles.excerpt: My obsession through the pandemic gave me the courage to play pick up basketball. This is that story, told one game at a time.

articles.title: What Jazz Taught Me About Trusting a Process You Can't Control
articles.excerpt: Jazz taught me to listen before I spoke, to trust the space between notes, and to find structure inside chaos. Turns out, faith works the same way.
